title: FLS Expanded Video Tutorial Series: Task 5 - Intracorporeal Suture
description: St. John Surgical Residency FLS Expanded Video Tutorial Series: Task 5 - Intracorporeal Suture

title: FLS Skills- Laparoscopic Intracorporeal Suturing:Fundamentals and Tips & Tricks for New Learners
description:
Medical Students and Residents will be encouraged to use this video to learn laparoscopic intracorporeal knot tying. This is a challenging skill to learn, but once mastered, most other laparoscopic task come with ease.

The outline of the video is as follows:

• Introduction
• Needle Anatomy
• Needle Entrance (proper loading and back-loading)
• Needle Handling (Swiveling)
• Needle Loading 
• Throwing a Stitch
• Knot-Tying (2 techniques, drop needle and hold needle)
• Tips and Tricks/Key Points

title: Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery (FLS) Tips and Tricks Task 5 Suture with intracorporeal knot
description:
Again, would recommend using 2-0 silk suture and same caveat of the actual testing center needle being smaller diameter than the one used here but same principles apply. 
Couple notes:
You can see the loop slipping out for my first attempted throw, this can be mitigated by careful movement of the grasper and by keeping the jaws open, also with the end of the needle pointing towards the camera. 
You can also see difficulty grasping the tip then pulling through at some points due to the needle dri

title: FLS (and beyond) Intracorporeal knot - Tips and Tricks
description:
Voted worldwide the slickest way ever to tie laparoscopic knots.
Shows basic FLS two-handed technique, as well as the more commonly used one-handed technique.

****How to remember the important details of laparoscopic knot tying****
Imagine you're responsible for wedding two people together. You are helping them ...tie the knot. It's crucial that: 
1. "Be on time for the wedding". If forming the loop with your right hand, make sure it is pointing to the 10 o'clock position (or to the 2 o'clock i

title: FLS Tutorial Intracorporeal Suture
description:
FLS Tutorial - Intracorporeal Suture made with Medisimu - the laparoscopic box trainer
Red More: https://medisimulator.com/product/suture-pad/

title: Exercise L-5: running suture
description: Standing on one side of the trainer box, participants used a running suture to approximate a 5.4-cm longitudinal fenestration in a 9.5-cmr-long, and 1.9-cm-wide (when flattened), thick-caliber Penrose drain. The Penrose, with five pairs of 2-millimeter diameter, black targets, each one 3 mm from the fenestration and spaced at 0.95 cm intervals. The suture, a braided 2-0 polygalactin 910, swedged on to a 26-mm, ½ curved tapered needle, was 20 cm long with an anchoring knot located 18 cm from the

title: LAPAROSCOPIC SURGERY BASICS #2 | INTRACORPOREAL TYING: COMMON MISTAKES
description:
Here we demonstrate a few common pitfalls you might encounter while tying intracorporeal knots during laparoscopic surgery. 

Remember: there are no absolute rules, only principles. 

Like and subscribe if you found this useful! Leave us a comment letting us know what you'd like to see next!

title: Intracorporeal Sliding Knot
channel: Maxmiliano Maricic MD - Ped. Surgery & Simulation
description:
A short video about the correct technique of the intracorporeal Sliding Knot.
Practicing in reduced working area, with different types of suture is essential to achieve proficiency of correct technique of the knot. It is a fundamental knot to approximate distant tissues or/and with some tension or thick structures.
Key exercise for training minimally invasive surgery.

title: Tumble Knot Tutorial
channel: Nationwide Children's Hospital Program in Minimally Invasive Surgery
description: Demonstration of intracorporeal suturing using the "tumble knot" technique. Also discussed are common pitfalls of intracorporeal knot tying.

title: Laparoscopic Intracorporeal Suturing

title: intracorporeal laparoscopic suturing instructions
description: An illustration for intracorporeal laparoscopic suturing

title: FLS Suturing Simulator (Intracorporeal Knot Tying) Demo
description:
This video shows a virtual simulator for the suturing with intracorporeal knot-tying procedure, side-by-side with the one performed in the real FLS box.
This simulator was developed by researchers with CeMSIM at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute including Drs Trudi Qi, Karthik Panneerselvam, Woojin Anh, Nick Milef, and Suvranu De, as well as Drs Sreekanth Artikala and Andinet Enquobahrie at Kitware Inc. The simulator was reviewed by Dr. Steven Schwaitzberg M.D. from University at Buffalo.

title: Lap101 series, FLS: Intracorporeal knot tying
description: Lap101 series, FLS: Intracorporeal knot tying. SimVivo practice kit (relatively affordable, 400$ for everything) & supplies from Limbs&Things (expensive - don't recommend unless you want FLS-specific training).

title: Intracorporeal Suturing & Knot Tying
description:
Intracorporeal Suturing is a fundamental core skill in minimally invasive surgery, it is an essential skill for advanced laparoscopic procedures.
In this video the technique is demystified and broken down to systematic and easy to follow steps.

title: LAPAROSCOPIC SURGERY BASICS #1 |  TYING AN INTRACORPOREAL KNOT
description:
UPDATED VIDEO: https://youtu.be/-YWqGy_jBXU 
Contains instructions on how to set up an above-the-knot tie. 

Watch as we walk you through one approach to tying the laparoscopic square knot. 

Remember: there are no absolute rules, only principles. 

Like and subscribe if you found this useful! Leave us a comment letting us know what you'd like to see next!

title: Exercise L-4: intracorporeal tie
description: The participant is positioned and provided laparoscopic scissors and the same choices of needle drivers or grasper offered in Exercise L-3 (see Video 4, http://links.lww.com/xxx). The exercise goal was to pass a 15-cm 2-0 polygalactin 910 suture (Vicryl), swedged on to a 26-mm ½ curved tapered needle through the marks on the Penrose and approximate the defect with a knot comprising three intracorporeally formed throws, the first of which was a double throw —a surgeon’s knot. The maximal allowed

title: Tips and Tricks for Laparoscopic Suturing and Knot Tying: Advancing Your Skills (Didactic)
description:
Tips and Tricks for Laparoscopic Suturing and Knot Tying: Advancing Your Skills (Didactic)
New learners, medical students and residents will be encouraged to use this video to learn laparoscopic ıntracorporeal knot tying.

title: Laparoscopyboxx app - Suture 2
description:
Get introduced to one of the laparoscopic sutuing techniques.
The Szabo technique is a technique in which the needle is not used during the formation of the knot. This decreases the chance of causing damage to the surrounding structures.

title: Laparoscopic intracorporeal suturing techniques - Best Laparoscopy hospital, Hyderabad
Laparoscopic intracorporeal suturing; 2nd step for beginners
description:
Basic laparoscopic suturing training for drybox with English subtitle. This movie includes basic concepts and some advanced skills.
Because I am not a native speaker of English, please no flames about my bad English.
Also please tell me more good phrases on this movie.

title: Endotrainer Exercises for Laparoscopic Skills | Dr. Makam Ramesh – BEST Institute
description:
Dr. Makam Ramesh demonstrates a series of laparoscopic endotrainer exercises focused on suturing and intracorporeal knotting techniques. These training drills help surgeons develop precision, hand-eye coordination, and proficiency in laparoscopic suturing.

Endotrainer practice is an essential part of laparoscopic skill development and allows surgeons to refine knotting and suturing techniques in a controlled training environment.

For Educational purpose only.
Property of B.E.S.T. Institute & R

title: How to tie Mishra's Knot for Laparoscopic Surgery
description:
http://www.laparoscopyhospital.com
For the surgeon to develop the same level of proficiency and dexterity in the endoscopic environment as he may possess in open surgery is not a simple matter. The use of proper Mishra's Knot, are essential.  Participating in an in-depth, systematic training program in a laboratory setting is essential before applying endoscopic Mishra's Knot techniques to humans. Successful acquisition of these Mishra's Knot skill requires that the surgeon be motivated to succe

The step-by-step LapPass technique for secure laparoscopic knot tying
